Petition Re: (1) Removal Of Successor Trustee Michael F. Hirner; (2) Appointment Successor Trustee; (3) Compel Surrender Of Trust Property To Successor Trustee; (4) Order Award Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioner; (5) Surcharge Michael F. Hirner For Professional Fees And Costs; (6) Compel Trustee Accounting; And (7) Violation Of California Welfare And Institutions Code Section 15610 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Feb-22-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-302717 Estate of Vishwas Narurkar and
PTR-19-302798 The Vishwas Narurkar Trust dated January 24, 2019.

Appearances: Monica Amboss and Alberto Campain appeared by zoom for Leela Narurkar and Arvind Narurkar.
Henry York appeared by zoom for Michael Hirner.

The Court noted that a joint status statement was just filed on Friday and that looks like the parties are one third to forty percent of the way through discovery. Mr. Campain stated that they conducted written discovery, that they are in the middle of depositions, that he believes they will be ready for trial in August of this year, and requested that the estate and the trust be tried at the same time. Mr. York stated that he is in agreement that they should ready for trial. The Court raised the issue of a motion for summary judgment and expressed concern about the reservation of rights. Mr. York stated that filing of a motion for summary judgment would depend on status of discovery and that it would put them somewhere in July. Mr. Campain expressed concern about his Clients being elderly and requested that a trial date be set today for a date in late September/October. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the First Amended Petition Re: Removal of Successor Trustee, etc. are set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on October 4, 2021 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court). The parties are to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Re: (1) Removal Of Successor Trustee Michael F. Hirner; (2) Appointment Successor Trustee; (3) Compel Surrender Of Trust Property To Successor Trustee; (4) Order Award Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioner; (5) Surcharge Michael F. Hirner For Professional Fees And Costs; (6) Compel Trustee Accounting; And (7) Violation Of California Welfare And Institutions Code Section 15610 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Aug-24-2020 Continued To Oct-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition
Off Calendar Aug-19-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Monica Amboss appeared by zoom for the moving party.
Alberto Campain appeared by telephone for the moving party.
Henry York appaered by zoom for Michael Hirner (Respondent).

Mr. Campain stated that he submits on the tentative ruling. Ms. Amboss stated that she agrees with the tentative ruling. Mr. York stated that he did not contact the Court to advise that he will contest the tentative ruling, but he does not agree with it, that he is fairly new to the BAR and that he was not notified of the tentative ruling. The Court allowed argument. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirms its tentative ruling as the final ruling.
The Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition is granted, specifically as to leave to file the Tenth Cause of Action as presented in the moving papers and counsel's supporting declaration. Petitioners are ordered to file and serve their Second Amended Petition within two days of entry of this order. While the Court is sympathetic to Respondent's frustration, granting the motion will not prejudice Respondent, and denial would deprive Petitioners of the right to assert a facially-valid cause of action. However, based on declaration of counsel, delay in bringing this motion for leave to amend was not due to Petitioners' action or inaction, but through the oversight of their attorneys. Pursuant to CCP section 473, as a condition of leave to amend, should Respondent file a motion for summary judgment to the amended pleading, Petitioners' attorneys are ordered to compensate Respondent $500 for the consequences of this belated amendment, equal to the filing fee for a motion for summary judgment. The moving party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition Re: (1) Removal Of Successor Trustee Michael F. Hirner; (2) Appointment Successor Trustee; (3) Compel Surrender Of Trust Property To Successor Trustee; (4) Order Award Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioner; (5) Surcharge Michael F. Hirner For Professional Fees And Costs; (6) Compel Trustee Accounting; And (7) Violation Of California Welfare And Institutions Code Section 15610 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Jun-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-19-302717 Estate of Vishwas A. Narurkar and
PTR-19-302798 The Vishwas Narurkar Trust.

Appearances: Richard Lund for Michael Hirner (Trustee/nominated Executor).
Monica Amboss for Leela Narurkar and Arvind Narurkar.

Mr. Lund stated that he scheduled summary judgment motions ('MSJ") in each matter and that he tried to get them heard on the same date, but he got two different dates. Mr. Lund also stated that the May 20th date conflicts with the date of the civil trial, asked that both MSJ's be heard on April 8th and that if not, set the hearing dates 30-days out. Ms. Amboss questions whether it makes sense to hold two different hearings when the facts are identical. Discussion was held as to how the Court's calendars are managed. The hearing dates on the motions for summary judgment remain the same. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the First Amended Petition Re: Removal of Successor Trustee are continued to June 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a joint status report in both matters indicating what claims are going to trial, any disputes of fact, who the witnesses will be, and time estimates of witness testimony, and the overall time estimate of the trial.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

Petition Re: (1) Removal Of Successor Trustee Michael F. Hirner; (2) Appointment Successor Trustee; (3) Compel Surrender Of Trust Property To Successor Trustee; (4) Order Award Attorney'S Fees And Costs To Petitioner; (5) Surcharge Michael F. Hirner For Professional Fees And Costs; (6) Compel Trustee Accounting; And (7) Violation Of California Welfare And Institutions Code Section 15610 Et Seq.
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were called: PES-19-302717 The Estate of Vishwas Narurkar and
PTR-19-302798 The Vishwas Narurkar Trust.

Appearances: Richard Lund for Michael Hirner (Executor).
Monica Amboss for Arvind Narurkar and Leela Narurkar (Decedent's Parents).

The Court informed Counsel that courtesy copies of all filed documents are to be delivered to the Court. Counsel reported that discovery is still ongoing. Mr. Lund stated that he is still planning on filing a motion for summary judgment within two weeks and that his goal is to have it heard by Christmas. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Removal of Successor Trustee, etc. are continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a status report as to where things stand, the length of trial, the number of witnesses, the number of exhibits, etc. by January 13, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
